rows.title: How Cole Outsmarted the Mermaid
paragraphs: Cole and the raccoon had arrived at the classroom with a simple plan: have a regular afternoon. The raccoon was good at regular afternoons.
The mermaid declared the birthday cake forbidden. Cole felt overexcited about this development. Possibly more overexcited than the mermaid had bargained for.

Cole tiptoed sideways while holding the shopping cart. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back. Everything in the room had picked up a faint teal tint. Just then a "wubba" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it.
"rubber duck!" said Cole. The rule developed a small visible crack. Nobody could see it, but everyone agreed it was there. The mermaid handed over the birthday cake without comment.
Heading home, Cole explained the loophole to the raccoon one more time. The raccoon was nodding seriously, clearly planning to deploy it later. Knowledge: transferred.

rows.title: Cole's Big Show
paragraphs: There was going to be a show. Cole had been planning it for days. The stage was the beach. The co-star was the sloth. The crucial prop was a magic eight ball.
Right on cue, the magic eight ball did the WRONG thing. It launched itself off the table, bounced once, and landed in the sloth's lap. The sloth looked at Cole like, "Well now what?"
Cole took a breath, sprinted forward, and shouted whatever came to mind: "bonk!" The audience leaned in. Sometimes nonsense lands. Meanwhile, some tacos waited patiently for its moment.

Cole pointed at the broken magic eight ball and shouted "bonk." The magic eight ball reassembled itself in roughly the wrong order. The sloth applauded the wrong order. The show was now better. The sunset orange thing was happening again, whatever it was. The whole day had a worried energy to it. Nobody could explain why. A talking sandwich watched the whole thing happen and offered no comment.
Curtain down. Cole high-fived the sloth. "splat?" said Cole. "splat!" said the sloth. The new catchphrase had landed.

rows.title: Who Took the Pancakes?
paragraphs: At the backyard, Cole set the pancakes down for one second. The lynx watched. Cole turned to grab a napkin. Just one second.
No pancakes anywhere. Cole went clumsy like a tiny detective. The yeti hummed a song nobody had asked for.

Right then the lynx hiccuped. Out came a tiny crumb of pancakes. Then another. Then a third. The lynx looked at the crumbs. The crumbs looked back. Everybody knew. For exactly two seconds, Cole charged like it was a job. There was a midnight blue feeling to the moment that nobody really named.
Cole looked straight at the yeti and said one word: "thwip." The yeti confessed immediately. To a different crime. Cole noted it for later.
Bedtime came. Cole yawned. The lynx yawned too. Just before sleep, the lynx mumbled "flumpy" into the pillow, like it was the official sign-off now.

rows.title: How Cole Tried to Sneak Past the Watchful One
paragraphs: At the living room, Cole told the unicorn the plan. The plan was simple: sneak past the watchful one. The unicorn nodded immediately. It was on.
The substitute teacher stood between Cole and victory. Cole felt sleepy about it. The substitute teacher did not look like it was going to move on its own.

Cole walked up to the substitute teacher in full sleepy mode. Not asking permission. Not apologizing. Just sleepy. The substitute teacher had not prepared for sleepy. The substitute teacher took a small step back. Meanwhile, some waffles waited patiently for its moment.

"rubber duck!" said Cole. The substitute teacher heard it, made a small noise, and stepped aside in a way that looked rehearsed. Cole snuck past the watchful one. The unicorn applauded the substitute teacher, because manners. Halfway through, Cole cartwheeled for absolutely no reason. There was a moss green feeling to the moment that nobody really named. "soggy sock," went somebody. Nobody asked who.
On the way home, Cole replayed the moment everything turned around. The unicorn kept bouncing along, already ready for whatever was next. Win logged. Onward.

rows.title: How Cole Outsmarted the Stone Giant

The stone giant declared the sushi forbidden. Cole felt wobbly about this development. Possibly more wobbly than the stone giant had bargained for.

Cole charged sideways while holding the apology balloon. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back. Cole noticed a streak of rainbow across the wall. Or did they. Just then a "grumblepoof" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it.
"paper crown!" went Cole. The stone giant heard a code word it apparently knew, mumbled "ah, the paper crown clause," and waved Cole through with the sushi. There was no paper crown clause.
Heading home, Cole explained the loophole to the tiger one more time. The tiger was nodding seriously, clearly planning to deploy it later. Knowledge: transferred.

rows.title: Cole vs the Troll

There was just one problem: a troll stood between Cole and the goal. "Nope," said the troll. "Not today. Not find the best possible snack. Not on my watch."

Cole walked up to the troll in full worried mode. Not asking permission. Not apologizing. Just worried. The troll had not prepared for worried. The troll took a small step back. Somebody had brought warm croissant. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.

"jiggly blorp!" said Cole. The troll heard it, made a small noise, and stepped aside in a way that looked rehearsed. Cole found the best possible snack. The turtle applauded the troll, because manners. Cole galloped without thinking. It was a thing they did now. Everything in the room had picked up a faint neon tint.
Cole climbed into bed. The turtle climbed in too. The day got its closing line: "mystery jelly," whispered Cole. The turtle sneezed in approval.

rows.title: Cole's Big Show
paragraphs: There was going to be a show. Cole had been planning it for days. The stage was the bus. The co-star was the lynx. The crucial prop was a map covered in crumbs.
Then everything went wrong. The map covered in crumbs broke. Actually broke. In half. Cole froze. The lynx froze. The pillows watched.

Cole took a breath, soared forward, and shouted whatever came to mind: "jiggly blorp!" The audience leaned in. Sometimes nonsense lands. For a beat the whole place looked weirdly mint green. Later someone would swear they heard "fwoosh" in the rafters. Underneath everything, Cole was running on pure cozy.

"jiggly blorp!" yelled Cole, pointing at the broken map covered in crumbs. The map covered in crumbs twitched. Twitched again. Then it did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it. Off to the side, a warm croissant sat there, mostly forgotten, definitely still part of the day. A witch watched the whole thing happen and offered no comment.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the lynx. The show was unrepeatable, which felt right. Time to pack up and find the next thing.

On the way home, Cole replayed the moment everything turned around. The wolf kept bouncing along, already ready for whatever was next. Win logged. Onward.
